NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul></ul><h6>Unordered Tetrad Two Gene Determine Linkage</h6><p>The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> has unordered tetrads. A cross is made to study the linkage relationships among two genes.</p> <p>Using the table above, determine the linkage between the two genes.</p>	The two genes are <strong style="color: #8B0000;">UNLINKED</strong> but may be on either the <strong style="color: #1E40AF;">SAME</strong> or <strong style="color: #D2691E;">DIFFERENT</strong> chromosomes.	Correct	The two genes are <strong style="color: #006400;">LINKED</strong> but are likely on <strong style="color: #D2691E;">DIFFERENT</strong> chromosomes.	Incorrect	The two genes are <strong style="color: #006400;">LINKED</strong> and on the <strong style="color: #1E40AF;">SAME</strong> chromosome. Incorrect	The two genes are <strong style="color: #5B2C6F;">NEITHER</strong> <strong>linked</strong> NOR <strong>unlinked</strong>.	Incorrect

A cross is made to study the linkage relationships among two genes.</p> <p>Using the table above, determine the linkage between the two genes.</p>	The two genes are <strong style="color: #7B3F00;">BOTH</strong> <strong>linked</strong> AND <strong>unlinked</strong>. A cross is made to study the linkage relationships among two genes.</p> <p>Using the table above, determine the linkage between the two genes.</p>	The two genes are <strong style="color: #7B3F00;">BOTH</strong> <strong>linked</strong> AND <strong>unlinked</strong>. Incorrect	The two genes are <strong style="color: #8B0000;">UNLINKED</strong> but may be on either the <strong style="color: #1E40AF;">SAME</strong> or <strong style="color: #D2691E;">DIFFERENT</strong> chromosomes. A cross is made to study the linkage relationships among two genes.</p> <p>Using the table above, determine the linkage between the two genes.</p>	The two genes are <strong style="color: #5B2C6F;">NEITHER</strong> <strong>linked</strong> NOR <strong>unlinked</strong>. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #206f3b;">Gene E</span> cells absorb excessive amounts of liquid, resulting in giant, swollen cells.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #1f5cb1;">Gene M</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #1f5cb1;"><i>militant</i></span>' phenotype. NPD &rarr; linked; PD &approx; NPD &rarr; unlinked</li></ul></ul><h6>Unordered Tetrad Two Gene Determine Linkage</h6><p>The yeast <i>Saccharomyces cerevisiae</i> has unordered tetrads. A cross is made to study the linkage relationships among two genes.</p> <p>Using the table above, determine the linkage between the two genes.</p>	The two genes are <strong style="color: #5B2C6F;">NEITHER</strong> <strong>linked</strong> NOR <strong>unlinked</strong>.	Incorrect	The two genes are <strong style="color: #006400;">LINKED</strong> and on the <strong style="color: #1E40AF;">SAME</strong> chromosome.	Correct	The two genes are <strong style="color: #8B0000;">UNLINKED</strong> but may be on either the <strong style="color: #1E40AF;">SAME</strong> or <strong style="color: #D2691E;">DIFFERENT</strong> chromosomes.	Incorrect	The two genes are <strong style="color: #006400;">LINKED</strong> but are likely on <strong style="color: #D2691E;">DIFFERENT</strong> chromosomes.	Incorrect	The two genes are <strong style="color: #7B3F00;">BOTH</strong> <strong>linked</strong> AND <strong>unlinked</strong>.	Incorrect

A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #4b6821;">Gene K</span> cells grow in twisted, coiled shapes, resulting in a knotted or gnarled appearance.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #4e48c9;">Gene M</span></strong> is analogous to the '<span style="color: #4e48c9;"><i>militant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #785800;">Gene J</span> colonies appear dotted with tiny, iridescent spots that sparkle under light.</li> <li><strong><span style="color: #4e48c9;">Gene M</span></strong> is related to the '<span style="color: #4e48c9;"><i>militant</i></span>' phenotype. A budding yeast that is homozygous recessive for <span style="color: #4e48c9;">Gene M</span> colonies are small, dense, and secrete compounds that inhibit the growth of nearby colonies.</li> </ul></p><table style="border-collapse: collapse;
